output.summary: Set EventHero minimum height to min(700px, 100vh) and align content to the bottom of the hero area. CSS-only and class changes — no attribute or block.json modifications needed.

fileChanges.description: Add min-height to .event-hero-inner and flex layout for bottom alignment
fileChanges.details: Add `min-height: min(700px, 100vh)` to `.event-hero-inner`. Add `display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: flex-end;` so the content layer is pushed to the bottom of the hero. This keeps the gradient and background image covering the full min-height while content sits at the bottom.

fileChanges.description: Update content layer div classes to work with new flex bottom-alignment
fileChanges.details: On the `.event-hero-inner` div (line 216), add classes or keep as-is if CSS handles it. On the content layer div (line 242), replace the large top padding `pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] sm:pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+280px)]` with a simpler top padding that just clears the header (e.g., `pt-[var(--header-height)]`) since the flex justify-end on the parent now pushes content to the bottom. The bottom padding `pb-8 sm:pb-16` should remain to give breathing room at the bottom.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the same class changes from EventHero.tsx
fileChanges.details: On the `.event-hero-inner` div (line 83), keep the same classes. On the content layer div (line 107), replace `pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] sm:pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+280px)]` with the same simplified top padding used in TSX. Must stay in sync with TSX.

designDecisions.decision: Use min(700px, 100vh) as the minimum height for the hero inner container
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested min(700px, 100vh) — same change being applied to Hero, PostHero, and EventHero blocks for consistency.

designDecisions.decision: Use CSS flexbox with justify-content: flex-end on .event-hero-inner to align content to the bottom
designDecisions.evidence: User requested content aligned to the bottom. Flexbox column with justify-end is the standard approach. Applied via CSS on .event-hero-inner rather than utility classes to keep it centralized.

designDecisions.decision: Replace large top padding with minimal header-clearing padding since flex justify-end handles vertical positioning
designDecisions.evidence: Current large pt values (180px/280px + header-height) were used to push content down. With flex justify-end, content naturally sits at the bottom, so only a pt that clears the fixed header is needed. A min-top-padding (pt-[var(--header-height)]) ensures the background image area above content still has the header clearance.
